headline: Bitcoin ETF Inflows Hit Record High as BTC Briefly Touches $50K
overview: Crypto markets rallied on January 15 as spot Bitcoin ETFs attracted their largest single-day inflow on record, surpassing $500 million. Bitcoin briefly crossed the $50,000 level during Asian trading hours before settling slightly lower. Ethereum ETFs recorded their second-highest inflow day since approval, reflecting broad institutional appetite across the crypto asset class. On-chain data showed continued accumulation by long-term holders, reinforcing the bullish near-term narrative.

key_events.title: Spot Bitcoin ETF Inflows Exceed $500M
key_events.significance: Record single-day inflows into spot Bitcoin ETF products signal accelerating institutional demand and further legitimise Bitcoin as an investable asset class.

key_events.title: Bitcoin Briefly Crosses $50,000
key_events.significance: Touching a key psychological price level attracts media attention and may draw additional retail and institutional interest, reinforcing momentum.

key_events.title: Ethereum ETFs See Parallel Inflow Surge
key_events.significance: Strong Ethereum ETF inflows on the same day as Bitcoin confirm that institutional appetite extends across the major crypto assets, not just Bitcoin.

market_context: Broader risk appetite was supported by stable US equity markets. The DXY dollar index held near recent highs, which typically creates a mild headwind for crypto; the resilience of inflows despite this context underlines conviction among buyers.
source_confidence_note: High confidence: multiple specialist outlets corroborated by real-time market data.
